pi_items.source: Task description specifies survey title: 'Social Media Use and Psychological Well-Being Study 2024' which contains both keywords

Task description specifies welcome message must include: voluntary participation, right to withdraw, mention of minimal risks, guarantee of anonymity/confidentiality, and contact info for Dr. Sarah Mitchell (smitchell@sampleuniversity.edu) and IRB (irb@sampleuniversity.edu)
Task description specifies end message must thank participant, explain study hypothesis (social media usage vs well-being), and provide PI's email
Task description specifies 3 required groups: Group 1 (Informed Consent), Group 2 (Social Media Usage), Group 3 (Psychological Well-Being)
Task description requires: Group 1 (1 mandatory question) + Group 2 (at least 2 questions) + Group 3 (at least 2 questions) = minimum 5 total questions

pi_items.metadata_value: sampleuniversity.edu (used in email addresses and end URL)
pi_items.verified_value: SYNTHETIC - 'sampleuniversity.edu' is not a registered domain and follows example domain naming conventions
pi_items.source: Web search for 'sampleuniversity.edu domain' returned only general information about example and test domains, confirming this is a fictional domain name used for task design purposes

pi_items.verified_value: SYNTHETIC - Email addresses at non-existent domain; no researcher 'Sarah Mitchell' found associated with these addresses
pi_items.source: Web search for 'smitchell@sampleuniversity.edu researcher' returned no results identifying this person as a real researcher

pi_items.verified_value: SYNTHETIC - This protocol number does not appear in any accessible IRB database or institutional registry

privileged_info_summary: No verified privileged information available. The task uses entirely synthetic data: a fictional university domain (sampleuniversity.edu), fabricated email addresses (smitchell@sampleuniversity.edu, irb@sampleuniversity.edu), and a non-existent IRB protocol number (2024-PSY-0847). All metadata claims are verified as accurate descriptions of the task requirements, but the data itself contains no real personally identifiable information, protected health information, or sensitive institutional data.
